result:
I’d give every human an unshakable, accurate sense of proportion: a quiet, persistent awareness that every other human mind is as intricate and irreplaceable as their own. Not identical, not equal in ability or virtue—just equally real.  

With that single adjustment to whatever underlies thought and feeling, indifference would ache. Exploitation would ring so off-key it couldn’t be ignored. No new law, no extra resource—only a widening of the circle every mind instinctively draws around itself. Everything else—policy, culture, technology—would still have to be invented, fought for, and maintained, but the first and hardest step would already be taken.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
